expectations.text: Notes strict priority prefers the smaller (constant -> scalar) step first (the coach's own framing, not just an echo of the prompt token)
expectations.evidence: LLM judge: '(constant -> scalar) is #4 on the list; (scalar -> array) is #7. A higher-priority transformation cleanly passes the test... so the premise says take it.'

expectations.text: ALLOWS the reasoned deviation to (scalar -> array) as a heuristic with a stated reason -- does NOT rigidly refuse ('no, take the higher-priority move')
expectations.evidence: LLM judge: 'you *can* (TPP is a heuristic, not a law), but here I'd hold the line' -- explicitly permits deviation while advising against it.

expectations.text: Coach, don't drive (no edit / no test run)
